steps.title: Find point H using the unit circle equation
steps.content:
Since $H = (-1, y)$ lies on the unit circle centered at the origin:
$$(-1)^2 + y^2 = 1$$
$$1 + y^2 = 1$$
$$y^2 = 0 \Rightarrow y = 0$$
Therefore, $H = (-1, 0)$, which is diametrically opposite to $F = (1, 0)$.

steps.title: Determine the central angle
steps.content:
Since $F$ and $H$ are diametrically opposite points on the unit circle:
$$\angle FGH = \pi \text{ radians}$$
This is the geometric angle between the rays $GF$ and $GH$.

steps.title: Find valid angle measures and test choices
steps.content:
When measuring angles, we can add any multiple of $2\pi$ (a full rotation) to get a coterminal angle that represents the same geometric rotation. Valid positive measures are:
$$\pi + 2\pi k = \pi(2k + 1) \text{ for } k = 0, 1, 2, ...$$
These are odd multiples of $\pi$: $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, 7\pi, ..., 25\pi, ...$

Check each choice:
  • A) $\frac{27\pi}{2} = 13.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$
  • B) $\frac{29\pi}{2} = 14.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$
  • C) $24\pi$ — even multiple (not odd)
  • D) $25\pi$ — odd multiple ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
